directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Which country has shown the maximum percentage change in FEII in 1998 relative to 1997?
explanation: FEII change 1997→98: India 1.71→0.72 ≈ −58% (the biggest change), Thailand 1.5→2.1 = +40%, Malaysia 2.1→2.43 ≈ +16%, Korea 2.8→3.12 ≈ +11%, China 5.46→4.8 ≈ −12%. The largest percentage change is India’s. Hence (a).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If China’s FEI is 10 times that of India in the year 1998, which of the following is true for 1998?

options.label: a
options.text: China’s GDP is 70% more than India.

options.label: b
options.text: China’s GDP is 40% more than India.

options.label: c
options.text: China’s GDP is 50% more than India.

explanation: FEII = FEI/GDP, so FEI = FEII × GDP. In 1998, FEI(China)/FEI(India) = (4.8 × GDP_China)/(0.72 × GDP_India) = 10 ⇒ GDP_China/GDP_India = 10 × 0.72/4.8 = 1.5. So China’s GDP is 50% more than India’s. Hence (c).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If GDP of India increased by 5%, GDP of China increased by 7% and that of Korea decreased by 2% from year 1997 to 1998, which of the following is/are true? I. FEI to China was higher in 1998 than in 1997. II. FEI to China was lower in 1998 than in 1997. III. India’s FEI has increased from 1997 to 1998. IV. FEI to Korea was lower in 1998 than in 1997. V. Korea’s FEI has increased in 1998 from 1997.
explanation: Take 1997 GDP = 100 for each; 1998 GDP = China 107, India 105, Korea 98. Using FEI = FEII × GDP: China 4.8×107=513.6 < 5.46×100=546 (FEI fell → II true); India 0.72×105=75.6 < 1.71×100=171 (III false); Korea 3.12×98=305.8 > 2.8×100=280 (FEI rose → V true, IV false). So II and V. Hence (b).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Which of the following can be inferred from the given data?

options.label: a
options.text: China’s GDP is more than India.

options.label: b
options.text: China’s GDP is less than India

options.label: c
options.text: India’s GDP is less than Malaysia

explanation: All figures are FEII = FEI/GDP ratios, so absolute GDP values cannot be determined or compared. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: In how many countries has the FEI increased from 1997 to 1998?
explanation: Only the FEII ratios are known, not GDP, so the change in FEI cannot be determined. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: What was the average annual growth rate in production for all four sectors combined between 89 and 98?
explanation: Apart from a few years in manufacturing and mining & quarrying, the yearly changes for all four sectors stay close to the 5–6% mark, so the average annual growth rate is about 6%. The other options (1.2%, 56.6%, 104%) are far off. Hence (b).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: When during the given period, was the maximum production level in the electricity sector achieved?
explanation: Every yearly change for Electricity is positive (5,7,8,10,5,6,8,9,6), so its index rises each year and is highest in the last year, 98. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: When, during the given period, was the minimum production level in the manufacturing sector achieved?
explanation: Manufacturing’s index = 100 in 1989 and rises from 1990 onward (+4 in 90, etc.), so the minimum production level is in 1989. Hence (a).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: For the given period, which sector showed the highest growth rate?
explanation: Cumulating the yearly % changes from 1989 to 1998, Electricity grows the most (index ≈ 185, i.e. ≈ +85%), more than Chemicals (≈ +68%), Manufacturing (≈ +46%) and Mining & Quarrying (≈ +31%). Hence (a).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: In 92, the index for the chemical sector as compared to the index for the Electricity sector was
explanation: Index in 92: Chemicals = 100×1.07×1.03×1.06 ≈ 116.8; Electricity = 100×1.05×1.07×1.08 ≈ 121.3. Chemicals is lower by about 5. Hence (b).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: By what percentage was the output of the manufacturing sector more than that of the chemical sector in 91?
explanation: Using the 1989 weights (Manufacturing 20, Chemicals 10): in 91, Manufacturing = 20×1.04×1.01 ≈ 21.0 and Chemicals = 10×1.07×1.03 ≈ 11.0. Manufacturing exceeds Chemicals by (21.0−11.0)/11.0 ≈ 91%. Hence (b).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: When did the maximum percentage change in the production levels of the mining and quarrying sector take place?
explanation: The yearly % changes for Mining & Quarrying are 5, −1, −2, 7, −4, 3, 5, 7, 8; the largest single-year change is +8% in 98, i.e. over 97–98. Hence (d).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If the total work force was 76 million whereas the total value added was 225 million, then which of the following had the maximum value addition per worker?
explanation: Value addition per worker = (Value Added %)/(Employment %): Central 10/6 ≈ 1.67, State 3/6 = 0.5, Central/State 12/3 = 4.0, Public 8/8 = 1.0. The maximum is Central/State. Hence (c).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Which of the following sectors has the maximum fixed capital invested per factory?
explanation: Fixed capital per factory = (Fixed Capital %)/(Factories %): Central 5/8 = 0.63, State 4/4 = 1.0, Central/State 5/6 = 0.83, Public 6/12 = 0.5. The maximum is State. Hence (b).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If the variable is proportional to the number of employees and the production per employee, then for which of the following is the production highest?
explanation: Production tracks the number of employees and output per employee. The Private sector has by far the largest share of employment (65%) and of value added (62%), so its production is the highest. Hence (b).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: If the government has a fixed capital of \$200 million in the Iron & Steel industry, which corresponds to 20.012% of its total investment as fixed capital, then how much did the government invest (in ₹ million) in Maruti Udyog Ltd which forms 25% of the investment in the joint sector? (1 US \$ = ₹45)
explanation: Government fixed capital in Iron & Steel = \$200 million = 20.012% of its fixed capital ⇒ Government fixed capital ≈ \$1000 million. That is 14% of the economy’s total fixed capital ⇒ total ≈ \$7143 million. Joint sector = 8% ≈ \$571 million; Maruti = 25% of joint = \$142.86 million = ₹(142.86×45) ≈ ₹6500 million. Hence (a).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Maruti Udyog Ltd is a joint project of the Indian Government and Suzuki Motors Japan, each having equal stake. One fine day, the Indian government decides to disinvest from the venture due to losses occurring from labour problems. How many money will be disinvested?
explanation: Maruti’s investment = 25% of the joint sector’s fixed capital ≈ \$142.86 million ≈ ₹6500 million (at ₹45/\$). This is the amount disinvested. Hence (b).

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: Which of the following statements is true?

options.label: a
options.text: The number of government employees is more than that of the number of factories in the joint sector

options.label: b
options.text: The number of employees in the public is same as fixed capital of joint sector

explanation: The grid gives only percentages of different totals (factories, employees, capital, etc.), so absolute numbers cannot be compared across columns. Neither statement can be confirmed. Hence (d).

context_passage:
Five women decided to go shopping to M.G. Road, Bangalore. They arrived at the designated meeting place in the following order: 1. Archana, 2. Chellamma, 3. Dhenuka, 4. Helen, and 5. Shahnaz. Each woman spent at least ₹1000. Below are some additional facts about how much they spent during their shopping spree:

i. The woman who spent ₹2234 arrived before the lady who spent ₹1193.

ii. One woman spent ₹1340 and she was not Dhenuka.

iii. One woman spent ₹1378 more than Chellamma.

iv. One woman spent ₹2517 and she was not Archana.

v. Helen spent more than Dhenuka.

vi. Shahnaz spent the largest amount and Chellamma the smallest.
directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: What was the amount spent by Helen?
explanation: Solving the clues: Chellamma ₹1139 (smallest), Dhenuka ₹1193, Helen ₹1340, Archana ₹2234, Shahnaz ₹2517 (largest). Helen spent ₹1340. Hence (b).

question_text: Which of the following amount was spent by one of them?
explanation: From the solution {1139, 1193, 1340, 2234, 2517}, the amount ₹1139 (Chellamma) was spent by one of them; the other listed amounts were not. Hence (a).

explanation: In the solution, ₹1193 was spent by Dhenuka (she arrived 3rd, after Archana who spent ₹2234). Hence (c).

question_text: Defining the profitability as the ratio of net profit to sales, IVP Ltd, recorded the highest profitability in

directions: Go through the data set given below and solve the questions based on it.
question_text: With profitability as defined in question 24, it can be concluded that:

options.label: a
options.text: Profitability is non-increasing during the five years from 1994–95 to 1998–99.

options.label: b
options.text: Profitability is non–decreasing during the five years from 1994–95 to 1998–99.

options.label: c
options.text: Profitability remained constant during the five years from 1994–95 to 1998–99

explanation: Profitability over the five years is 2.5%, 1.8%, 2.0%, 2.93%, 1.76% — it rises and falls, so it is neither non-increasing, nor non-decreasing, nor constant. Hence (d).
